feedback: The analysis is comprehensive enough to approve: it clearly identifies the gap in the develop stage, maps the required prompt/model/pipeline/reviewer changes, defines measurable acceptance criteria, and covers the main operational risks around e2e execution, placement, timeout, and schema compatibility. The strongest remaining weakness is that some acceptance criteria assume every issue can always produce a meaningful e2e test, and the optional fast-fail execution path is not resolved into a firm recommendation.
concerns: Acceptance criterion requiring at least one e2e test for every develop_node run may be too absolute; some changes may be docs-only, config-only, or otherwise unsuitable for true e2e coverage.
The analysis leaves unresolved whether e2e tests are actually executed in develop_node or only checked by MR review, which affects implementation scope and product behavior.
The proposed mitigation to mock external services in e2e tests may weaken the definition of end-to-end testing unless clear boundaries are documented.
No concrete verification plan is listed for testing the prompt/schema/MR-description changes themselves.
Raising developer_timeout_seconds is proposed as a mitigation but not included in the technical approach or acceptance criteria.
suggestions: Define when e2e tests are mandatory versus when an explicit justification is acceptable.
Make the post-develop pytest execution decision explicit instead of optional.
Add implementation-level tests for DeveloperOutput parsing, MR description rendering, and revision prompt propagation.
Clarify the minimum acceptable definition of e2e for this project, such as CLI-level, public API-level, or full pipeline-level coverage.
Include how skipped e2e tests should be handled so the system does not accept placeholder or non-executing tests as adequate coverage.
